chain_edges.notes: Apr 26 hyperscaler-lab compute coupling sets up Apr 27 contract anchors that focal cites as new doctrine.
Codesigned hyperscaler-lab exclusives upstream of routing lab compute through hyperscaler silicon.
FOMC + Mag 7 print window same-day backdrop; weak temporal co-occurrence only.
GGUF SSTI evidence (SGLang CVE-2026-5760) feeds the regulatory-primitive case for an AI-Infra KEV sub-catalog.
An AI-Infra KEV sub-catalog plus OWASP Loader-RCE top-3 normalizes adjacent OWASP MCP Top 10 publication.
Existing codesigned exclusives (MS-OpenAI, Amazon-Anthropic) are the very contracts the focal prediction expects to see publicly amended.
Capital-coupling lock-in pressure is the precondition the AGI-clause unwind responds to by converting capability triggers into calendar-bound caps.
AI-capex ROI reset and tech-stock pressure push hyperscalers to restructure frontier-lab anchor deals into calendar-bound revenue caps.

relations.notes: Both predict a Big-3 hyperscaler publicly gating GPU capex on per-token-margin floor by Q3 2026; focal restates with Apr 27 contract anchor.
Both lean on Apr 27 OpenAI/Microsoft + Anthropic/AWS Trainium pacts; focal targets ROI disclosure, sibling targets serving-topology lock-in.
Three-cloud training oligopoly is the structural backdrop for hyperscaler capex-gating doctrine.
Hyperscaler-lab codesigned exclusives provide the silicon-routing premise focal builds capex-disclosure thesis on.
Same-day siblings both anchored on Apr 27 OpenAI/Microsoft pact restructuring.
Both forecast a CISA/NVD AI-Infra sub-catalog plus OWASP Loader-RCE top-3 from the same SGLang/Antigravity/simple-git stack.
Same claim shape: NIST/NVD AI-Infra CVE tag plus OWASP top-3 Loader RCE by Q3 2026 from the inference-runtime CVE cluster.
Defensive hub-side response (HF/Ollama signing, vLLM SSTI scan) parallels regulatory KEV/OWASP track from the same SGLang root.
Inference-server KEV sub-catalog and OWASP MCP Top 10 are adjacent regulatory tracks within the agent-runtime security theme.
Third-party-vendor SOC 2 / FedRAMP baseline and AI-Infra KEV sub-catalog are independent facets of frontier-AI security tightening.
Same hyperscaler-frontier-lab contract cluster: af6 sees 5-pole exclusivity lock-in, focal sees one contract publicly amended toward multi-cloud.
Both treat hyperscaler-lab capital coupling but disagree on direction: 24c expects compute-stack lock-in, focal expects at least one unwind to multi-cloud.
81d focuses on three-cloud oligopoly for training capacity; focal focuses on contract-clause restructuring within those alliances.
Different facets of the frontier-lab commercialization arc: closed-weights re-expansion vs contract-clause unwind toward multi-cloud distribution.
